feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Data Services
feed.subtitle: SAP Data Services Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Which SAP Data Services( BODS) 4.3 versions supports RHEL 8.6 or 8.9 ?
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We need clarification on below points, kindly do the needful.

1. We want to install SAP BODS on RHEL 8.9 OS, could you please let us know if this is supported?

2. If we choose OS RHEL 8.6 or RHEL 8.9 (without SAP Solution flavor), Does SAP provide the required repository and SAP hardening document which we can apply on plain SAP OS before installing SAP BODS?

Regards, 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Where does SAP BODS belong in SAP's new groups of products?
entry.content:
Is

SAP BODS dying? SAP has partnerd with Syniti for data migrations,. 

Where does SAP BODS belong in SAP's new groups of products?

entry.title: Need to read BODS repository for job information
entry.content: In my BODS job, I need to read the last successful run of that job, to get the last succesful run  timestamp information. How can we achieve this in BODS job?

entry.title: How do I best organize large Data Services jobs for reliability?
entry.content:
We have about 2000 data flows currently organized in about 500 work flows and 50 jobs.  The jobs are run using ActiveBatch, an enterprise scheduling tool.  Although some of the objects are sequential due to required prerequisites, most of them are simply designed to spread out and organize the load.

Currently some of the largest tables that take hours to load have their own job.  Other jobs are small and can be rerun easily.  But some jobs have a large number of data flows and/or large tables and take a long time to run.  When these have an error, it's complicated, as we don't always have time to rerun the job.  So I'm looking for ways to reorganize things to recover more easily; I need to get as much data as possible loaded as fast as possible, without rerunning more than is necessary and with the ability to move past unresolvable errors.

There are two main scenarios: (1) transient errors such as memory, in which case the job can usually be rerun successfully, and (2) data issues, in which case the job would fail again.  In the ideal world we could just use recovery mode, but it needs an option to either skip or rerun the failed objects.  And we have not been able to get it to work with ActiveBatch anyway.

For scenario 2, in most cases we would rather have the job skip over the bad data and continue and load everything possible.  It seems to do this when the table is loaded in bulk mode, but that is not an option with template tables.  I tried using overflow files but that didn't help.  There ought to be a setting for jobs/workflows to continue in the case of failure.  There are options like like try/catch blocks and status tables, but that would take a lot of work.

One option is to put more data flows into a single workflow and letting the max number of processes regulate it.  Since a job won't fail until the workflow is complete that the failed data flow is in, this would allow everything to complete except the one that failed.  This would be good for scenario 2 but bad for scenario 1 as we'd have to rerun everything.  I am dumbfounded that DS doesn't have a setting to regular the number of processes at the server level though.  

The ideal way would be to put everything into its own job and manage it in ActiveBatch, but that is a different group and they wouldn't be too happy about it.  But I think I probably need to break things up into more jobs one way or another.

So I'm wondering what your strategies are.  Thanks.

entry.title: bods job failed- Cache statistics not avalable
entry.content:
Hi All

A recurring job failed with below error , please advise

 

Regards-Siba

entry.title: SAP Data Services Designer 4.2 and Windows Server 2019 compatibility

Hi 

Is SAP Data Services Designer 4.2 is compatible with Windows Server 2019?.

entry.title: Different SAP Data Services versions for UAT and PROD environment feasibility
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Current we are using a separate Product key for SAP Data Services for UAT and PROD environment. As we have SAP data service 4.2 for PROD and SAP data service 4.3 for UAT .Due to the downtime complications, we are unable to upgrade our PROD instance to SAP data service 4.3

We want to verify if it is feasible to keep two different Product Keys one for UAT testing and other for PROD environment.

Also, confirm even if the same is feasible, there will be no compliance issue for the same.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: How to find the SAP Data Services Installation Guide for Unix
entry.content:
Hello Team,

Can you please provide the complete documents for installing the SAP Data Services for the version 4.2 and 4.3 which is installing on SUSE Linux Server.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: A DS Job taking very long time
entry.content:
Hi All

 

A DS Job taking 11-18 Hours to complete and it retrieves arounds 115550635 row count.

What are the areas needs to be checked to reduce the execution time.

 

BR

sIBA

we want to download latest SAP data service version, could you please provide us the link to download? thank you.

Best regards

entry.title: SAP data services connection with MySQL 8.X
entry.content:
Need to connect SAP data services to MySQL 8.X 

Question and Answer:
Q: Please categorize your issue by choosing the option below that best fits your scenario.
A: Database datastores

Q: What version of SAP Data Services are you using?
A: 4.2

Q: What is the database type and version? And what is the database client version installed on your server?
A: sybase 16


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Rep Schedule in DS Mgmt Console
entry.content:
Hi All

Please anyone let me know whats the function of Repository Schedules in DS Management Console

 

BR

Siba

entry.title: BODS v4.3 performance issue with SQL Server
entry.content:
Hello,

We recently upgraded to BODS v4.3 and since then the job has taken almost 5 times more than usual to complete for Full load.

I checked on BODS v4.2 and it used to take around 8 hours to complete the full load.

We are using SQL Server database.

entry.title: Issue with JSON File creation as an Output
entry.content:
We are planning to create an output JSON file through BODS which is using a target template attached in the email below, reading data from relational table.
Issues we are facing 
  1. We are unable to keep the names to be exactly the same for all of the Hierarchical Data nodes (ArticleGroupID,ParentArticleGroupId, ArticleGroupDescription ) in the output file as we receive errors complaining about naming them different.
  2. Output structure is coming out the way we want, but it is repeating itself number of times source rows are there 
     
Source Query result is shown in color coding for the various levels deep in the data and finally it's the leaf.
 
Output File Template
{
  "ArticleGroup": [
    {
      "ArticleGroupID": "PD00009125",
      "ParentArticleGroupId": null,
      "ArticleGroupDescription": "Digital Product Game",
      "ArticleGroup": [
        {
          "ArticleGroupID": "PD00016135",
          "ParentArticleGroupId": "PD00009125",
          "ArticleGroupDescription": "Integration Game",
          "ArticleGroup": [
            {
              "ArticleGroupID": "PD00023331",
              "ParentArticleGroupId": "PD00016135",
              "ArticleGroupDescription": "Integration Game - Mobile",
              "ArticleGroup": [
                {
                  "ArticleGroupID": "PD00020400",
                  "ParentArticleGroupId": "PD00023331",
                  "ArticleGroupDescription": "Integration Game - Mobile - City Builder",
                  "Articles": [
                    {
                      "ArticleID": "PD00020401",
                      "ArticleDescription": "Integration Game - Mobile - City Builder - Ad Supported"
                    },
                    {
                      "ArticleID": "PD00020402",
                      "ArticleDescription": "Integration Game - Mobile - City Builder - Cosmetics"
                    },
                    {
                      "ArticleID": "PD00020403",
                      "ArticleDescription": "Integration Game - Mobile - City Builder - Crypto / NFT"
                    }
                  ]
                }
              ]
            }
          ]
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

entry.title: SAP Data services
entry.content:
Hello Team,

We are currently using SAP Data services 4.2 SP 14 Patch 5 and OS RHEL 7.9

Since RHEL 7.9 will end on 30th June, upgrading the OS to RHEL 8.9.

My question is will SAP Data services 4.2 SP 14 Patch 5 work on RHEL 8.9

Please provide your inputs 

Thank you

entry.title: "Failed to copy DSConfig.txt" message appearing during silent install
entry.content:
I'm using the below batch file to install SAP Data Services 4.2

start /wait %~dp0setup.exe -q InstallDir="c:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects\" SelectedLanguagePacks="en" ProductKey=XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX DSConfigASSelection=skip DSConfigCMSSelection=skip DSConfigMergeSelection=skip ISCommonDirChanged=0 Features=DataServicesClient,DataServicesDesigner,DataServicesDataDirect,DataServicesDocumentation

It seems to install fine but shows a popup at the end which says "Failed to copy DSConfig.txt from 'c:\program files (x86)\SAPBusinessObjects\Data Services\conf' to '\conf'.  Copy the file manually after installation completes."

I specified -q in the install so it should be suppressing all popups.  How do I prevent this message?

entry.title: PG 16 compatibility with DS 4.3
entry.content: We would like to know from which DS 4.3 version (which SP/Patch) there will be compatibility with PostgreSQL 16 (currently only compatible with PG 15 according to PAM)?

entry.title: Data services job taking 6-9 Hrs
entry.content:
Hi Team,

A recurring jobs running for every 4 hours. But each of it's execution took 6 to 9 Hrs to complete successfully. Because of this, many times some executions are running in parallel i.e., if one execution not finished in 4 Hrs , then the next execution will start after 4 Hrs so there will be  2 executions runs in parallel which could impact performane as well as end user not getting data on time. So kindly suggest on this.

Regards

Siba
